attributes.description: Alerts when pods are in Failed phase (phase == 4). Failed pods have terminated with an error and will not be restarted. May indicate persistent issues requiring operator intervention.
attributes.name: [K8s OTel] Pods in Failed phase

investigation_guide.blob:
## Pods in Failed Phase

### Triage Steps
1. Identify the affected pod(s) from the alert results.
2. Check the Cluster Overview dashboard for an increase in Failed phase pods.
3. Get pod details: `kubectl describe pod <pod_name> -n <namespace>`.
4. Check container logs: `kubectl logs <pod_name> -n <namespace> --all-containers --previous`.
5. Review pod events for scheduling failures, image pull errors, or resource issues.

### Common Causes
- All containers in the pod exited with non-zero exit codes
- Eviction due to node resource pressure
- Preemption by higher-priority pods
- Job pods that failed their task

### Tuning
- Pods in Failed phase from completed Jobs are expected; exclude Job namespaces if they cause noise.
- Add namespace filters to focus on production workloads.

### Escalation
Failed pods that belong to Deployments or StatefulSets should trigger replica replacement. If they don't, the controller may be stuck — investigate the controller logs.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-k8sclusterreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE k8s.pod.phase IS NOT NULL
| STATS observed_phases = VALUES(k8s.pod.phase)
    BY k8s.pod.name, k8s.namespace.name, k8s.node.name
// Phase 4 = Failed — alert if any data point in the window showed Failed,
// even if the pod has since transitioned to another phase
| WHERE MV_CONTAINS(observed_phases, TO_LONG(4))
| EVAL status = "Failed"
| KEEP k8s.namespace.name, k8s.pod.name, k8s.node.name, status, observed_phases
| SORT k8s.namespace.name ASC, k8s.pod.name ASC
| LIMIT 100
